  %2 = load i32, ptr %count, align 4
  %3 = add i32 %2, 1
  store i32 %3, ptr %count, align 4
  %4 = load ptr, ptr %current, align 8
  %5 = load ptr, ptr %4, align 8
  store ptr %5, ptr %current, align 8
  br label %loop

exit:
  %6 = load i32, ptr %count, align 4
  ret i32 %6
}

CLASS_1.name: Structural/Syntactic Failures
CLASS_1.description: Violations of basic LLVM IR syntax and SSA form

sub_categories.1.1: SSA violation: reuse of %name, multiple definitions
sub_categories.1.2: Incomplete syntax: unclosed blocks, missing terminators
sub_categories.1.3: Malformed type annotations: wrong integer width, float mismatch
sub_categories.1.4: Invalid instruction format: wrong operand count, illegal opcode
sub_categories.1.5: Code fence artifacts: markdown or text mixed into IR

sub_categories.2.1: Missing basic block labels or duplicate labels
sub_categories.2.4: Loops approximated/simplified rather than faithfully reconstructed

CLASS_3.name: Type System Failures
CLASS_3.description: Type mismatches and incorrect type usage

sub_categories.3.1: Type mismatch in operations (e.g., add i64 on i32 operands)
sub_categories.3.2: Pointer vs value confusion (wrong load/store types)

CLASS_4.name: Semantic/Functional Failures
CLASS_4.description: Valid IR that computes wrong result

sub_categories.4.1: Wrong computation despite valid IR structure
sub_categories.4.2: Heuristic pattern matching (plausible but incorrect IR)

CLASS_5.name: Scale/Context Failures
CLASS_5.description: Failures related to input size and context limitations
